message:
test(fcp-core): pin Hash-then-Equality contract on canonical IDs (llp14)

Add crates/fcp-core/tests/hash_eq_contract.rs pinning the Rust Hash
contract — `k1 == k2 → hash(k1) == hash(k2)` — on the five canonical
fcp-core identifier types: ZoneId, CapabilityId, InstanceId,
ObjectId, and quorum::NodeId.

For each type, equal values are constructed via different paths
(factory / try_from / FromStr / from_static / Display roundtrip /
clone / String vs &str / lowercase vs uppercase hex) and a fresh
DefaultHasher (the same hasher backing HashMap) verifies they hash
to the same u64. A HashMap-keyed sanity round-trip pins that this
holds when the types serve as HashMap keys.

9 tests pass. Drift in the underlying representation (e.g. switching
String ↔ Arc<str> backing, [u8;32] ↔ Vec<u8> for ObjectId, or adding
a new private field that derives Hash but not Eq) shows up here.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
